skill.name: context-management-context-save
skill.description: Guides AI agents through saving project context, decisions, state summaries, and reusable handoff notes.
skill.summary: Create structured context capture plans for long-running AI work.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, sample schema text, and descriptive phrases as command execution, network use, and weak cryptography. Manual review found no executable scripts, no real network call, no prompt injection attempt, and no evidence of weak cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The reported locations are Markdown inline code markers, input parameter names, and fenced code block delimiters. No evidence found of Ruby backtick execution, shell invocation, or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each reported location was inspected and contains Markdown formatting or documentation examples only. The skill has no script file, command runner, or executable shell instruction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: JSON Schema URL Flagged as Network Access
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is the standard JSON Schema identifier inside an example schema. No evidence found of an HTTP client, fetch call, webhook, download, or data transmission.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only as schema metadata in a documentation example. It is not used by code and cannot make a network request by itself.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain the skill description, a context management overview sentence, and a compression-mode sentence. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found descriptive prose only. There is no algorithm name or code path that implements weak cryptography.

content.user_title: Save Project Context for AI Handoffs
content.value_statement: Long AI work can lose decisions, project state, and rationale between sessions. This skill guides structured context capture so Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can resume work with less rework.
actual_capabilities: Defines what project context should be captured before a handoff.
Suggests storage formats such as structured JSON, Markdown, Protocol Buffers, MessagePack, and YAML.
Guides capture of architectural decisions, dependencies, semantic tags, and project metadata.
Outlines context compression, drift detection, and versioning strategies.
Describes workflows for onboarding, long-running sessions, and context restoration.
limitations: Provides guidance and examples, not a complete executable context storage system.
Does not include working integrations for vector databases or embedding services.
Requires users to exclude secrets and sensitive files during context capture.
Needs project-specific customization for schemas, tags, and storage policies.

use_cases.title: Prepare a project handoff
use_cases.description: Capture key decisions, open tasks, dependencies, and risks before another AI agent or teammate resumes work.
use_cases.target_user: Developers using multi-session AI coding workflows

use_cases.title: Create onboarding memory
use_cases.description: Build a structured summary of architecture, conventions, and project state for faster ramp-up.

use_cases.title: Track context drift
use_cases.description: Compare current project assumptions with saved context to identify outdated decisions or missing updates.

prompt_templates.title: Capture a basic handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner context save for a small project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a concise context save for the current project. Include project purpose, current state, open tasks, key files, and known risks.

prompt_templates.title: Document architecture decisions
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate capture focused on technical rationale
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to save architectural context for this repository. Focus on decisions, tradeoffs, dependencies, constraints, and areas that changed during this session.

prompt_templates.title: Design a reusable context schema
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for repeatable context storage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a reusable context schema for this project. Include required fields, optional metadata, update rules, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan semantic retrieval
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for teams with vector search
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a semantic context retrieval workflow. Define what to embed, how to tag context records, and how to prevent stale or sensitive data from being reused.

output_examples.input: Save context for a feature branch before ending the session.
output_examples.output: A handoff summary with current branch state, completed work, unresolved issues, test status, and next recommended steps.

output_examples.input: Create a project memory plan for a new team member.
output_examples.output: An onboarding context outline covering project goals, architecture, important files, dependency notes, and decision history.

output_examples.input: Check whether saved context is still current after several code changes.
output_examples.output: A drift review that lists stale assumptions, new decisions to capture, and context records that should be updated.

best_practices: Define the capture scope before saving context.
Remove secrets, credentials, and private user data from saved context.
Version context records when project decisions or dependencies change.
anti_patterns: Saving entire repositories when a focused summary is enough.
Treating generated context as authoritative without verification.
Embedding sensitive operational details in shared handoff notes.

faq.question: Does this skill save files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for what to capture and how to structure the saved context.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is tool-agnostic and can support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it require a vector database?
faq.answer: No. Vector databases are described as an optional advanced retrieval approach.

faq.question: What should be excluded from saved context?
faq.answer: Exclude secrets, credentials, personal data, proprietary details, and any files outside the approved project scope.

faq.question: Is this a replacement for documentation?
faq.answer: No. It complements documentation by capturing current session state, rationale, and handoff details.

faq.question: How often should context be saved?
faq.answer: Save context before ending long sessions, changing major architecture, or handing work to another person or agent.
